prototype_structure_type: High-entropy monoclinic C2/m (ccp Cl, stacking faults)
rationale: High configurational entropy (5 M-cation species) promotes stacking faults and 3D diffusion vs. 2D in Li3InCl6. Ionic potential descriptor guides phase stability. 2.13 mS/cm > 1.27 mS/cm for Li3InCl6. Multiple additional HE compositions unexplored.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - halide SEs form LiCl+metal (In/Sc/Zr) upon Li contact; exothermic; LPSC protective interlayer required
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V within reductive window estimate; interlayer recommended
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 at ~4.3 V borderline; halide+LiNiO2 DFT reaction energies >100 meV/atom (ESTIMATED unfavorable)
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 at 4.2 V within oxidative window; chloride family compatibility well-established

rationale: Low Hf content (x=0.3) enhances conductivity 28% over Li3InCl6 without sacrificing stability window (2.68-4.22 V). Aliovalent substitution creates extra Li vacancies. ccp Cl sublattice with monoclinic C2/m. Excellent candidate for further optimization x=0.2-0.5.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - halide family In3+/Hf4+ reduction to metals+LiCl; requires LPSC or LiI interlayer
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V below reductive limit 2.68 V; anode-side interlayer required
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate-Good - LiNiO2 at ~4.3 V marginally above 4.22 V; halide+LiNiO2 DFT rxn energies >100 meV/atom
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 at 4.22 V within measured stability window; halide LCO compatibility established for family

reported_conductivity_mS_cm: 5.36 (experimental peak at x~4.5 in Li3YCl6-xBrx series)
rationale: Systematic ML-MD and experiments show maximum conductivity in Li3YCl6-xBrx at x≈4.5. Cl/Br site disorder significantly expands diffusion pathways versus pure endpoints. Band gap ~5.3 eV exceeds threshold. Composition is underexplored beyond endpoints.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - Y3+ reduction to Y+LiBr+LiCl confirmed by AIMD for Li3YClxBr; LPSC interlayer required
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V; reductive limit 0.59 V near graphite; LPSC buffer recommended
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor - oxidative limit ~3.15 V insufficient for LiNiO2 at 4.3 V
lico_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiCoO2 standard 4.0-4.2 V exceeds oxidative limit 3.15 V; lower voltage application only

activation_energy_eV: <0.30 likely; not exact in extracted table
rationale: Ge4+ substitution at P5+ introduces extra Li (Li6.6). 2.9 mS/cm—highest in the study comparing Cl/I argyrodites and Ge/Si variants. I- provides large polarizable anion. Band gap slightly below 3.5 eV threshold. Practical synthesis demonstrated. Good template for further Ge loading.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor thermodynamic - Li2S+LiI+Li3P SEI; kinetically stabilized; LiF-rich coating or LPSC interlayer required
graphite_compatibility: Good - graphite anode at 0.1-0.2 V within practical kinetic window; standard argyrodite strategy
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor - exceeds thermodynamic oxidative limit; kinetic passivation limited; cathode coating required
lico_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiCoO2 at 4.2 V within practical kinetic window; partial argyrodite oxidation at interface; 300-cycle retention demonstrated for Cl/Ge analogue

rationale: Si4+ introduces Li+ excess (charge balance) boosting carrier density. 1.7 mS/cm confirmed experimentally. I- anion keeps bottleneck wide. Lower cost than Ge variant. Underexplored in terms of optimal Si content beyond x=0.25.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor thermodynamic - sulfide argyrodite family reduction; Li2S+LiI+Li3P SEI; kinetically limited
graphite_compatibility: Good - graphite anode standard for argyrodites; within kinetic window
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor without coating - same as Cl/Br variants; thermodynamically unstable
lico_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiCoO2 practical window cycling; related Ge-I analogue data extrapolated

prototype_structure_type: LiMXCl4 non-close-packed (1D MX2Cl4 octahedral chains, Pnma-related)
band_gap_eV: >5 (F introduces larger gap than O analogues)
rationale: LiMXCl4 family exhibits soft-cradle effect: MX2Cl4 octahedral chains tilt in concert with Li hops. LiHfFCl4 Li-stuffed variant predicted 137.5 mS/cm—highest in family. F-for-O substitution widens electrochemical window. E_hull 12-32 meV/atom depending on competing phases. Synthesis feasibility not yet proven experimentally.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - reductive limit 1.66 V; incompatible with Li metal; Li2HfCl6+LiCl decomposition; requires anode-side SE
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V below reductive limit 1.66 V; anode-side electrolyte buffer required
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - oxidative 4.20 V borderline for LiNiO2 at 4.3 V; Cl- oxidation risk near upper cutoff
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 at 4.2 V within oxidative window; catholyte role viable

stability_window_V: ~0.5-3.5 (est. from Na3MX6 Br/I family; Na halide trends)
rationale: Mixed Br/I anion in C2/m monoclinic structure (same as Li3InCl6 topology but for Na). 7.5 mS/cm at 300 K predicted—highest in Na3MX6 series studied. Ea 0.21 eV. The Gd-I6 analogue gives 0.59 mS/cm; Br3I3 mix gives 13x improvement via disorder and bottleneck tuning. Na halide electrolytes remain far less explored than Li analogues.
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor - oxidative ~3.5 V insufficient for oxide cathodes >3.6 V
lico_o2_compatibility: N/A (Na system; analogous NaCrO2 cathode within window estimate)

rationale: Zr-analogue of LiHfFCl4. Zr is more earth-abundant. E_hull ~24-38 meV/atom (r2SCAN). Fluorination widens electrochemical window vs. LiZrOCl4. Soft-cradle octahedral tilting mechanism. High conductivity predicted.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - reductive limit 2.20 V; Zr4+ reduction to Zr+LiCl/LiF; incompatible with Li metal; anode-side SE required
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V below limit 2.20 V; anode-side interlayer required
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 at 4.3 V exceeds 4.06 V oxidative limit; borderline; Cl- oxidation risk
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 at 4.06 V within window; catholyte role viable

stability_window_V: ~0.5-3.5 (est. from Na halide Br/I family; Gd analogue)
rationale: Y analogue of GdBr3I3. 3.3 mS/cm predicted. Br/I disorder critical for mS/cm range. Y is more abundant and cheaper than Gd. C2/m structure with 3D Na+ pathways. Unexplored experimentally.
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor - oxidative ~3.5 V insufficient for high-V Na oxide cathodes
lico_o2_compatibility: N/A (Na system); NaCrO2-class cathodes within window estimate

prototype_structure_type: Monoclinic C2/m (ccp Cl; mixed Er/Sc cation disorder)
reported_conductivity_mS_cm: >1 (predicted from ionic potential analysis; ccp should yield ~mS/cm range)
rationale: Er3+ and Sc3+ have similar ionic radii. Mixed cation at M-site should promote ccp Cl stacking over hcp (analogous to Y1-xInxCl6 series). ccp sublattice is key for >1 mS/cm. Novel composition—literature shows Li3MCl6 for pure Er or Sc but not mixed. Ionic potential between 2.7-3.5 suggests C2/m phase.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - halide family reduction; Er3+/Sc3+ to metals+LiCl; LPSC interlayer
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 borderline at ~4.3 V; chloride family +Li3MCl6 DFT data
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 within window; chloride family validated

prototype_structure_type: Monoclinic C2/m (ccp Cl; In stabilizes ccp)
reported_conductivity_mS_cm: >1 (predicted; In drives ccp which enables mS/cm)
stability_window_V: 0.6-4.2 (est.; In drives ccp; Er-In chloride analogue)
ehull_meV_atom: <30 (estimated; In3+ drives ccp; Er3+ lanthanide stable)
rationale: In3+ forces ccp Cl sublattice (essential for high conductivity). Er3+ lanthanide provides additional cation site disorder. Li3MCl6 for pure Er (P-3c1) has low conductivity. Mixing In+Er retunes structure toward ccp. Analogous strategy proved successful for Y1-xInxCl6 reaching ~1 mS/cm at x≥0.5.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - halide reduction; In3+/Er3+ to metals+LiCl; LPSC interlayer
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite with LPSC interlayer viable
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 borderline at 4.2 V; DFT rxn energy unfavorable
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 at 4.2 V within window; In-chloride family validated

stability_window_V: 0.6-4.2 (est. from Zr-Cl halide family; Bi3+ concern)
ehull_meV_atom: <40 (estimated; Bi3+ introduces tetrahedral Li at vacancies)
rationale: Bi3+ substitution on Zr4+ introduces tetrahedral Li sites plus additional Li concentration. AIMD predicts 10.93 mS/cm with Ea 241 meV. Cooperative short-to-long range Li-Li migration demonstrated. Low-cost Zr base. 50% Br substitution further smooths energy landscape (tested computationally).
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - Bi3+ reduction by Li metal likely (low reduction potential); Zr4+ stable; Bi risk dominates; requires interlayer
graphite_compatibility: Moderate - graphite with interlayer; Bi reduction at anode risky without buffer
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 borderline; Bi doping uncertain effect on high-V stability
lico_o2_compatibility: Moderate-Good - LiCoO2 at 4.2 V likely compatible per Zr-Cl halide family; Bi concern noted

reported_conductivity_mS_cm: ~1 (conductivity near transition; increases with In content toward 1.7 at x=0.9)
stability_window_V: 0.62-4.21 (est. from Y1-xInxCl6 Cl family endpoints DFT)
rationale: Y1-xInxCl6 solid solution undergoes hcp->ccp structural transition at x≈0.3-0.5. Conductivity jumps from 6×10-5 (pure Y) to ~1 mS/cm once ccp achieved. x=0.5 is in the transition zone. Humidity tolerance strongly improved vs. pure In.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - halide family Y3+/In3+ reduction; LPSC interlayer required
lini_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiNiO2 borderline; DFT rxn energies unfavorable
lico_o2_compatibility: Good - LiCoO2 within window; chloride family validated

stability_window_V: 1.19-2.38 (DFT thermodynamic); practical kinetic argued wider but intrinsic limit confirmed experimental
rationale: Record sulfide conductor. 1D Li channels along c-axis. Metastable ~25 meV/atom. Band gap 3.6 eV meets threshold. Narrow electrochemical window limits anode compatibility.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor - severe Ge reduction to Li15Ge4+Li2S+Li3P; kinetically self-passivating but resistive interphase grows; LiH2PO4 surface treatment shown to improve to 950h stability
graphite_compatibility: Poor - graphite at 0.1-0.2 V below thermodynamic reductive limit 1.19 V; Li-In alloy anode at 0.6 V standard workaround
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor - thermodynamic oxidative limit 2.38 V; kinetic passivation claimed but degradation at interface occurs; cathode coating essential
lico_o2_compatibility: Poor - LiCoO2 at 4.0-4.2 V far exceeds thermodynamic limit; severe interface degradation confirmed by STEM/XPS; electrochemically driven mechanical failure

prototype_structure_type: Argyrodite (F-43m, mixed Cl/Br at X site)
ehull_meV_atom: ~10 (interpolated Cl and Br endpoints 12.6 and 5.2 meV/atom)
rationale: Mixed Cl/Br at argyrodite X site introduces compositional disorder in addition to S/halide site disorder. Expected to yield ~2-3 mS/cm. Near-hull ~9 meV/atom. Pressure dependence of activation volume studies done but bulk synthesis optimization at this composition is not well covered in literature.
li_metal_compatibility: Poor thermodynamic - Li2S+LiCl+LiBr+Li3P SEI; SEI kinetically limited; coating needed
graphite_compatibility: Good - graphite standard argyrodite strategy; within kinetic stability window
lini_o2_compatibility: Poor without coating - thermodynamically unstable; kinetic passivation limited
lico_o2_compatibility: Moderate - LiCoO2 practical window cycling; coating or LPSC cathode layer recommended
